title: 36-July 12-13
tags: N/A
description:
Marianne Vitale, b. 1973 USA at Giardini delle Vergini
Marianne Vitale creates monuments from the spectres of American industrial expansion – bridges, railroad tracks, freight train engines, dams, factories, outhouses – in an excoriating critique of the contemporary collapse of Western society. She burns, breaks, bruises, and builds anew, forcing us to interrogate our own histories through the objects we abandon in the name of progress. For The Milk of Dreams, Vitale presents two series of works in a secluded garden part of the Giardino delle Vergini. For fifteen years, Vitale has constructed scale models of North American bridges in her studio, taking them outdoors to burn them and eventually exhibiting their charred skeletons. Installed here are seven of her now-iconic burned bridges cast in bronze. If the bridges portray the ruptured connection nodes of a climate-ravaged future, Bottle People (2020–2021), a series of liquor bottles swaddled in fabric and cast in bronze, represents a bestiary of ancient microbial phantasms unleashed from the ashen earth. Dozens of these gesturing figures, suspended off walls as if levitating, portray agony, despair, hope and amusement.

title: 23-July 12-13, 2022 Venice No.3
tags: N/A
description:
History of Night and Destiny of Comets
Gian Maria Tosatti, b. 1980 Rome, Italy
History of Night and Destiny of Comets is an installation by Gian Maria Tosatti that combines literary references, visual art, theatre, and performance. It confronts the tricky balance between Humanity and Nature, between the dreams and mistakes of the past and the prospects future. The first part, History of Night, traces the rise and fall of the Italian industrial “miracle”, the vast warehouses between Ragusa and Cremona, the single paradoxically homogeneous panorama of a hypothetical journey into the Italian provinces that today reflects the frustration of a working-class that has come to an end. This scenario sets the stage for the epiphany of the last act, Destiny of Comets, that is, of humanity that has crossed the earth in a rapid and luminous trajectory without, in the end, being guaranteed the right to inhabit this planet forever. Here, imagination is overturned in a cathartic epiphany.

title: Pavillon de l'Italie (60è Biennale d'art de Venise)
tags: dalbera venise venice italie biennale biennaledevenise installation artcontemporain anniedalbera
description: To Hear / Entendre Dans la 2ème salle du pavillon de l'Italie se trouvent un orgue mécanique et d'autres tuyaux plus petits, placés au sein d'un immense échafaudage. Une musique d'orgue de Caterina Barbieri et Kali Malone est diffusée périodiquement. Au centre de la salle, un énigmatique bassin rempli d'un liquide gris subit d'étranges remous. L'ensemble est lui aussi propice à la méditation contemplative. Pavillon de l'Italie www.labiennale.org/en/art/2024/italy Producteur​: Angelo Piero Cappello Commissaire : Luca Cerizza Artiste : Massimo Bartolini massimodecarlo.com/artists/massimo-bartolini Lieu : Arsenale Photo : Annie Dalbéra

title: Pavillon de l'Italie (60è Biennale d'art de Venise)
tags: dalbera venise venice italie biennale biennaledevenise installation artcontemporain grandorgue
description: To Hear / Entendre Cette salle presque vide est la première que l'on visite quand on entre dans le pavillon de l'Italie. On y entend un bruit sourd très grave. Elle contient un gigantesque tuyau d'orgue (64') de 20 m placé au sol qui émet un grondement sourd et sur lequel est posée une statuette représentant "Le Bodhisattva méditant". Cette sculpture du Bouddhisme coréen du début du VIIè siècle ainsi que l'ambiance sonore incitent à la méditation et préparent à la visite de la 2ème salle où se trouvent un orgue mécanique et d'autres tuyaux au sein d'un immense échafaudage. Au centre, un énigmatique bassin rempli d'un liquide gris subit périodiquement d'étranges remous. Pavillon de l'Italie www.labiennale.org/en/art/2024/italy Producteur​: Angelo Piero Cappello Commissaire : Luca Cerizza Artiste : Massimo Bartolini massimodecarlo.com/artists/massimo-bartolini Lieu : Arsenale

title: Le Pavillon de la République Populaire de Chine (60è Biennale d'art de Venise)
tags: dalbera venise venice italie biennale biennaledevenise installation artcontemporain
description: Atlas : l'harmonie dans la diversité Le Pavillon de la République Populaire de Chine Les oeuvres exposées sont de grande qualité et constituent des relectures contemporaines du patrimoine culturel chinois comme indiqué dans la thématique générale du pavilllon. Elles sont individuellement très intéressantes mais la scénographie est d'une grande banalité et ne les met guère en valeur. Le journal du pavillon qui présente les oeuvres et les artistes exposés est par contre très bien fait et indispensable pour comprendre les intentions des commissaires. Le pavillon de la République Populaire de Chine entend démontrer un changement de paradigme – de la différence à la coexistence – en réactivant et en diffusant la sagesse ancrée dans la culture traditionnelle chinoise, qui prône l’harmonie dans la diversité, la coexistence harmonieuse et la beauté partagée. Producteur : China Arts and Entertainment Group (CAEG) Commissaires : Wang Xiaosong, Jiang Jun Artistes : Che Jianquan, Jiao Xingtao, Shi Hui, Qiu Zhenzhong, Wang Shaoqiang, Wang Zhenghong, Zhu Jinshi, The project team of “A Comprehensive Collection of Ancient Chinese Paintings” lieu : Arsenal www.labiennale.org/en/art/2024/china-people%E2%80%99s-rep...

title: Le Pavillon de la République Populaire de Chine (60è Biennale d'art de Venise)
tags: sculpture dalbera venise venice italie biennale 2024 biennaledevenise artcontemporain
description: Soul - Rhyme Sculpture de JIAO Xingtao (Chine, 1970) L'artiste a représenté des sculptures traditionnelles chinoises en utilisant de petites plaques de cuivre. En s'éloignant de la sculpture, on retrouve les formes canoniques car les discontinuités des plaques s'atténuent. Il a également modélisé ces sculptures en 3D pour pouvoir les animer. Neuf sculptures sont exposées devant un écran sur lequel des films de chaque sculpture en mouvement sont projetés. Pavillon de la République Populaire de Chine Atlas : l'harmonie dans la diversité Le pavillon de la République Populaire de Chine entend démontrer un changement de paradigme – de la différence à la coexistence – en réactivant et en diffusant la sagesse ancrée dans la culture traditionnelle chinoise, qui prône l’harmonie dans la diversité, la coexistence harmonieuse et la beauté partagée. Producteur : China Arts and Entertainment Group (CAEG) Commissaires : Wang Xiaosong, Jiang Jun Artistes : Che Jianquan, Jiao Xingtao, Shi Hui, Qiu Zhenzhong, Wang Shaoqiang, Wang Zhenghong, Zhu Jinshi, The project team of “A Comprehensive Collection of Ancient Chinese Paintings” lieu : Arsenal www.labiennale.org/en/art/2024/china-people%E2%80%99s-rep...

title: Le Pavillon de la République Populaire de Chine (60è Biennale d'art de Venise)
tags: dalbera venise venice italie biennale biennaledevenise peinturechinoise
description: Exemples de peintures chinoises traditionnelles inventoriées dans des collections et musées étrangers Cet écran complète une documentation sur le travail d'inventaire réalisé depuis plus de 10 ans concernant les peintures chinoises traditionnelles appartenant à des collections et musées situés hors de Chine. C'est la première partie de l'exposition du pavillon de la Chine. Les oeuvres d'artistes contemporains inspirés par le patrimoine artistique chinois en constituent la 2ème partie. Pavillon de la République Populaire de Chine Atlas : l'harmonie dans la diversité Le pavillon de la République Populaire de Chine entend démontrer un changement de paradigme – de la différence à la coexistence – en réactivant et en diffusant la sagesse ancrée dans la culture traditionnelle chinoise, qui prône l’harmonie dans la diversité, la coexistence harmonieuse et la beauté partagée. Producteur : China Arts and Entertainment Group (CAEG) Commissaires : Wang Xiaosong, Jiang Jun Artistes : Che Jianquan, Jiao Xingtao, Shi Hui, Qiu Zhenzhong, Wang Shaoqiang, Wang Zhenghong, Zhu Jinshi, The project team of “A Comprehensive Collection of Ancient Chinese Paintings” lieu : Arsenal www.labiennale.org/en/art/2024/china-people%E2%80%99s-rep...

title: "Le pavillon de l'Italie" à la Biennale de Venise 2022
tags: dalbera venise venice venezia italie biennale artcontemporain pavillonnational atelier usine machineàcoudre
description: l'Histoire de la nuit Première partie du pavillon de l'Italie : "Histoire de la nuit et destin des comètes" : l'installation restitue un atelier des années 1960 de fabrication de vêtements ou d'autres objets textiles, abandonné par ses personnels. Au premier étage, le bureau allumé est celui du contre-maître qui surveillait l'atelier. Il s'agit de l'évocation du déclin industriel de l'Italie. La deuxième partie de l'installation entièrement dans la pénombre évoque la fin de la civilisation (elle n'est pas photographiable). Installation de Gian Maria Tosatti (Rome, 1980) www.tosatti.org/BIO.html machines à coudre, chaises, tables et divers mobiliers d'une usine Biennale de Venise 2002, le pavillon de l'Italie est situé dans le site de l'Arsenal Commissaires : Onofrio Cutaia, Direction Générale de la Créativité Contemporaine, Ministère de la Culture Commissaire : Eugenio Viola l' Histoire de la nuit , retrace l'essor et le déclin du miracle industriel italien, l'étendue des entrepôts entre Raguse et Crémone, seul panorama paradoxalement homogène d'un hypothétique voyage en Italie provinciale qui reflète aujourd'hui la frustration d'une classe qui vient de se terminer. Ce scénario prépare l'épiphanie finale, le Destin des Comètes, c'est-à-dire de l'humanité qui a traversé la terre dans une trajectoire rapide et lumineuse, sans être assurée d'habiter cette planète pour l'éternité.... Extrait du site de la Biennale d'art 2022 www.labiennale.org/it/arte/2022/italia

title: Le pavillon de la République populaire de Chine (Biennale de Venise 2019)
tags: dalbera venise italie biennaledevenise2019 biennaledart pavillondelachine chenqi
description: A Place without Whence or Whither / Un lieu sans origine ni destination Le plafond de cet espace construit dans le jardin nord de l'Arsenal, à la sortie du pavillon national de la Chine, est percé de trous aux formes ondulées, à travers lesquels passe la lumière du soleil qui vient se refléter sur les murs et le sol du pavillon. Cet espace qui change tout au long d'une journée ensoleillée, incite à la contemplation et à la méditation, c'est l'oeuvre la plus simple mais aussi la plus subtile du pavillon chinois. Installation de l'artiste chinois Chen Qi (Chine, 1963) www.artsy.net/artist/chen-qi-chen-qi?redirectTo=/artist/c... 2019 Aluminium, acier L'espace n'est accessible qu'à 12 visiteurs en même temps, avec des protections sur leurs chaussures. Il fait l'objet d'un nettoyage régulier par une gardienne en permanence à l'entrée. Il est fermé en cas de pluie. Titre du pavillon : Re-睿 Arsenal Producteur : China Arts and Entertainment Group Ltd. (CAEG). Commissaire : Wu Hongliang. Artistes : Chen Qi, Fei Jun, He Xiangyu, Geng Xue. Le pavillon de la République populaire de Chine à la Biennale d'art de Venise 2019 www.labiennale.org/en/art/2019/national-participations/pe...

title: Forêt de balais de Michel Blazy (Biennale de Venise 2017)
tags: dalbera michelblazy venise venezia venice italie italy italia biennale vivaarteviva 57thinternationalartexhibition balais brooms scope
description: Forêt de balais / Broom Forest / Foresta di scope Installation de Michel Blazy (Monaco, 1966) 2013 / 2017 sorgho, sol, eau, balais Courtesy the Artist; Art: Concept, Paris Michel Blazy est un artiste conceptuel qui travaille avec le vivant, ce qui rend ses installations évolutives, car elles dépendent du temps qui passe et de leur environnement. Pour la Biennale, il a placé des balais fabriqués en paille de sorgho contenant des graines de sorgho au sein d'une parcelle du "Giardino delle Virgini" qui est mitoyen de l'Arsenal de Venise. Il en résulte une plantation un peu chaotique où le vivant reprend ses droits sur l'artiste. www.galerieartconcept.com/fr/artiste/michel-blazy/ oeuvre présentée dans le "Giardino delle Vergini", Venise Biennale de Venise 2017 www.labiennale.org/en/art/

title: Sculptures de Salvatore Arancio (Biennale de Venise 2017)
tags: dalbera venise venezia venice italie italy italia biennale vivaarteviva 57thinternationalartexhibition salvatorearancio

Oeuvres présentées dans le "Giardino delle Vergini", Venise Biennale de Venise 2017 www.labiennale.org/en/art/

title: Le projet Warka Water (Biennale d'architecture 2016, Venise)
tags: venise venezia venice biennaledarchitecture2016 biennaledivenezia dalbera labiennaledivenezia arsenale biennalearchitettura2016 warkawater
description: Le projet Warka Water a pour objectif de fournir une source d'eau potable alternative pour des populations rurales défavorisées. Le projet est développé sur les plateaux d'Ethiopie La tour, construite en matériaux locaux, est conçue pour capter l'humidité de l'air et stocker l'eau ainsi récoltée. L'architecte italien Arturo Vittori est à l'origine du projet. Un exemple de tour a été construit dans le cadre de la biennale d'architecture à l'Arsenal dans le jardin au nord de la darse Le site présentant le projet Warka Water www.warkawater.org/ Site de la 15ème biennale d'architecture de Venise 2016 (thème : Reporting from the front) www.labiennale.org/en/architecture/news/22-02.html

description: Walking Nimbus, oeuvres de Lu Yang LU Yang was born in 1984 in Shanghai, China and received her MFA in New Media Art at the China Academy of Art in 2010. She currently splits her time between Beijing and Shanghai and has become an increasingly important figure in the field of new media and biological art. Walking Nimbus 2015 / HD Film / Multimedia / 4'40'' In the ancient images and sculptures of deities, the nimbus symbolised divine wisdom held by the gods. It was also seen as an important feature that distinguished the gods from humans. In Walking Nimbus, individuals of all walks of life are invited to wear these “nimbuses” and become gods, of sorts, as they move around the space. Since an ancient time, understanding and perception of religious idols has been determined by human experience and shaped by regional elements. The artwork challenges the traditional forms of the “nimbus”, as it was originally used, posing such questions as: could the images of certain ancient religious icons become shared cultural heritage for all? Is religious symbolism to remain regionally and racially specific? Or can we, through a drastic reinterpretation of the use of ancient symbols, provide an all-encompassing experience for humankind? extraits du site officiel du pavillon de Chine Pavillon national de la République Populaire de Chine artistes du pavillon : LIU Jiakun, LU Yang, TAN Dun, WEN Hui / Living Dance Studio, WU Wenguang / Caochangdi Work Station Commissario: China Arts and Entertainment Group, CAEG. Commissario Aggiunto: Zhang Yu, Yan Dong. Curatore: Beijing Contemporary Art Foundation. description:
Di ritorno dall'ultimo ponte: anche stavolta ho viaggiato verso est, ho ritrovato tutte le sensazioni dei miei ritorni dopo un mese passato al mare in Veneto.
La latitudine, la luce, sono le stesse... eppure...
Eppure uscendo dall'autostrada ho visto questi viali alberati deserti all'ora di pranzo. Ombre nette e taglienti e facciate ammazzate dal sole. Parcheggi semivuoti con qualche lamiera colorata a scaldarsi per cuocere gli autisti del poi. Il nulla, la luce troppo forte, il sole, il tutto.
Questi in cui vivo abitualmente sono luoghi che non sono fatti per l'estate. Ci si dovrebbe fermare alla primavera per passare direttamente all'autunno.
L'estate con la sua luce e il suo sole appiattisce tutto, uccide l'essenza di luoghi di pianura che sono fatti per le nuvole e il cielo grigio. Il nulla e quando nulla non è, rimane fastidioso e stonato.
La pigrizia dei tavolini di un bar all'ora di pranzo. Un gelato preso “tanto per” in piazza in centro. Le vie strette che fermano anche gli unici colpi di vento che potrebbero portare o riportare vita, se ce ne fosse mai stata.
E senza neanche la consolazione di avere un mare vicino, a qualche chilometro.
L'estate uccide la pianura, uccide le città e i paesi.
L'estate qui, e sono ormai 34 estati, mi uccidono ogni anno sempre un po' di più.

description:
Venetian Arsenal - Arsenale di Venezia 
A complex of former shipyards and armories clustered together in the city of Venice in northern Italy. Owned by the state, the Arsenal was responsible for the bulk of the Venetian republic's naval power during the middle part of the second millennium AD. Construction of the Arsenal began around 1104, during Venice's republican era. It became the largest industrial complex in Europe prior to the Industrial Revolution, spanning an area of about 45 ha, or about fifteen percent of Venice. Surrounded by a 3,2 km rampart, labourers and shipbuilders regularly worked within the Arsenal, building ships that sailed from the city's port. With high walls shielding the Arsenal from public view and guards protecting its perimeter, different areas of the Arsenal each produced a particular prefabricated ship part or other maritime implement, such as munitions, rope, and rigging. These parts could then be assembled into a ship in as little as one day. An exclusive forest owned by the Arsenal navy, in the Montello hills area of Veneto, provided the Arsenal's wood supply. The Arsenal produced the majority of Venice's maritime trading vessels, which generated much of the city's economic wealth and power, lasting until the fall of the republic to Napoleon's conquest of the area in 1797.

description:
Biennale di Venezia 2014 - 14th International Architecture Exhibition - Fundamentals.
Fundamentals consists of three interlocking exhibitions: 
1.Absorbing Modernity 1914-2014 is an invitation to the national pavilions to show the process of the erasure of national characteristics. 
2.Elements of Architecture, in the Central Pavilion, pays close attention to the fundamentals of our buildings used by any architect, anywhere, anytime. 
3.Monditalia dedicates the Arsenale to a single theme – Italy – with exhibitions, events, and theatrical productions.

The 14th International Architecture Exhibition, titled Fundamentals, directed by Rem Koolhaas and organized by la Biennale di Venezia, chaired by Paolo Baratta, was open to the public from June 7 through November 23, 2014, in Venica Italy. 65 National Participations were exhibiting in the historic pavilions in the Giardini, in the Arsenale, and in the city of Venice. They examine key moments from a century of modernization. Together, the presentations start to reveal how diverse material cultures and political environments transformed a generic modernity into a specific one. Participating countries show, each in their own way, a radical splintering of modernity's in a century where the homogenizing process of globalization appeared to be the master narrative

Absorbing Modernity 1914–2014 has been proposed for the contribution of all the pavilions, and they too are involved in a substantial part of the overall research project, whose title is Fundamentals. The history of the past one hundred years prelude to the Elements of Architecture section hosted in the Central Pavilion, where the curator offers the contemporary world those elements that should represent the reference points for the discipline: for the architects but also for its dialogue with clients and society. Monditalia section in the Corderie with 41 research projects, reminds us of the complexity of this reality without complacency or prejudice, which is paradigmatic of what happens elsewhere in the world; complexities that must be deliberately experienced as sources of regeneration. Dance, Music, Theatre and Cinema with the programmes of the directors will participate in the life of the section, with debates and seminars along the six-month duration of the exhibition.

Elements of Architecture / Central Pavilions
This exhibition is the result of a two-year research studio with the Harvard Graduate School of Design and collaborations with a host of experts from industry and academia. Elements of Architecture looks under a microscope at the fundamentals of our buildings, used by any architect, anywhere, anytime: the floor, the wall, the ceiling, the roof, the door, the window, the façade, the balcony, the corridor, the fireplace, the toilet, the stair, the escalator, the elevator, the ramp. The exhibition is a selection of the most revealing, surprising, and unknown moments from a new book, Elements of Architecture, that reconstructs the global history of each element. It brings together ancient, past, current, and future versions of the elements in rooms that are each dedicated to a single element. To create diverse experiences, we have recreated a number of very different environments – archive, museum, factory, laboratory, mock-up, simulation.

1.Absorbing Modernity 1914-2014 is an invitation to the national pavilions to show the process of the erasure of national characteristics. 
2.Elements of Architecture, in the Central Pavilion, pays close attention to the fundamentals of our buildings used by any architect, anywhere, anytime. 
3.Monditalia dedicates the Arsenale to a single theme – Italy – with exhibitions, events, and theatrical productions.

The 14th International Architecture Exhibition, titled Fundamentals, directed by Rem Koolhaas and organized by la Biennale di Venezia, chaired by Paolo Baratta, was open to the public from June 7 through November 23, 2014, in Venica Italy. 65 National Participations were exhibiting in the historic pavilions in the Giardini, in the Arsenale, and in the city of Venice. They examine key moments from a century of modernization. Together, the presentations start to reveal how diverse material cultures and political environments transformed a generic modernity into a specific one. Participating countries show, each in their own way, a radical splintering of modernity's in a century where the homogenizing process of globalization appeared to be the master narrative

Absorbing Modernity 1914–2014 has been proposed for the contribution of all the pavilions, and they too are involved in a substantial part of the overall research project, whose title is Fundamentals. The history of the past one hundred years prelude to the Elements of Architecture section hosted in the Central Pavilion, where the curator offers the contemporary world those elements that should represent the reference points for the discipline: for the architects but also for its dialogue with clients and society. Monditalia section in the Corderie with 41 research projects, reminds us of the complexity of this reality without complacency or prejudice, which is paradigmatic of what happens elsewhere in the world; complexities that must be deliberately experienced as sources of regeneration. Dance, Music, Theatre and Cinema with the programmes of the directors will participate in the life of the section, with debates and seminars along the six-month duration of the exhibition.

Elements of Architecture / Central Pavilions
This exhibition is the result of a two-year research studio with the Harvard Graduate School of Design and collaborations with a host of experts from industry and academia. Elements of Architecture looks under a microscope at the fundamentals of our buildings, used by any architect, anywhere, anytime: the floor, the wall, the ceiling, the roof, the door, the window, the façade, the balcony, the corridor, the fireplace, the toilet, the stair, the escalator, the elevator, the ramp. The exhibition is a selection of the most revealing, surprising, and unknown moments from a new book, Elements of Architecture, that reconstructs the global history of each element. It brings together ancient, past, current, and future versions of the elements in rooms that are each dedicated to a single element. To create diverse experiences, we have recreated a number of very different environments – archive, museum, factory, laboratory, mock-up, simulation.

description: Le canal de Porta Nova et une des deux tourelles de style néogothique Ce canal qui donne accès à l'Arsenal a été aménagé entre 1809 et 1811 pour le passage de navires de grandes dimensions. --------- Défini par le gouvernement de la République comme le " coeur de l'état vénitien ", l'Arsenal fondé en 1104 par le Doge Falier, devint le plus puissant chantier naval du monde. Ceint par 3 km de murailles crénelées rouges, il a employé jusqu'à 16.000 personnes fr.wikipedia.org/wiki/Canale_di_Porta_Nuova Photo Annie Dalbéra

title: Le pavillon de la République populaire de Chine (Biennale d'architecture 2014, Venise)
tags: venice italy italia biennale venise venezia italie dalbera pavillondelachine biennaledarchitecture
description: La partie intérieure du pavillon de la République Populaire de Chine dans l'Arsenal de Venise Pour la 14è Biennale internationale d'architecture de Venise (2014), trois équipes d'architectes, d'artistes et de designers chinois ont été invitées à collaborer par le commissaire, pour réaliser le pavillon de la République Populaire de Chine intitulé : "Mountains beyond mountains". Comme l'ont mis en scène la plupart des pavillons nationaux pour leur propre pays, un panorama d'un siècle d'architecture en Chine (1914-2014) y est détaillé et illustré sous forme de photos, de plans, de maquettes et de vidéos. De grands projets d'aménagement comme celui du Pearl River Delta : PRD (Hong Kong, Macao, Shenzhen, Guangzhou...) y sont décrits. En tentant de faire le lien entre le passé et le futur de l'architecture en Chine, le message volontariste du pavillon chinois est que la réussite de la modernisation de la société passe avant tout par l'attention apportée au cadre de vie et à l'environnement. Les concepteurs peinent néanmoins à convaincre de cette volonté de continuité qui, on ne le sait que trop, est soumise en Chine aux impératifs idéologiques et économiques et se trouve dépendante des pratiques souvent corrompues des décideurs. Le pavillon est complété par une installation extérieure de couleur blanche, construite en matériaux recyclés et plutôt bien intégrée au jardin de l'Arsenal (voir les photos). Le pavillon de la Chine a été installé dès 2005 dans un des hangars de l'Arsenal qui, à l'époque de son fonctionnement, contenait les citernes de carburant. Jusqu'à l'an dernier, les citernes étaient encore visibles ce qui réduisait considérablement l'espace d'exposition de la Chine et diffusait en plus des odeurs nauséabondes de gas oil. Pour la biennale 2014, les citernes ont été supprimées, une seule a été conservée (à droite sur la photo) pour la mémoire du lieu. C'est un signe de l'importance croissante du rôle que les organisateurs entendent voir jouer à la Chine dans cette exposition internationale d'architecture qui est la plus importante au monde. L'UABB : Bi-City Biennale of Urbanism Architecture (Shenzhen-Hong Kong) participe à la programmation des évènements associés au pavillon de la Chine et au pavillon de la Finlande. En effet, la biennale d'architecture de Shenzhen est jumelée à celle de Venise et la cité de Shenzhen est elle-même jumelée à la Finlande. En 2014, la présence chinoise ne s'est pas limitée au pavillon officiel, car les villes de Macao et de Hong Kong disposent comme chaque année de leurs propres pavillons situés hors de l'Arsenal, plusieurs artistes chinois sont présents dans les expositions d'art qui se déroulent au moment de la Biennale et une exposition (Across chinese cities - Beijing) sur l'évolution d'un quartier de Beijing a lieu dans l'Arsenal nord dans le cadre des évènements collatéraux. Site officiel de la 14è Biennale internationale d'architecture de Venise www.labiennale.org/it/architettura/index.html CINA, Repubblica Popolare Cinese Mountains beyond Mountains Urbanus (Meng Yan, Wang Hui, Liu Xiaodu), Duoxiang Studio (Lu Xiang, Jia Lianna, Chen Long, Hu Xian), Open Architecture (Li Hu, Huang Wenjing), To Meet You graphics (Liu Zhizhi, Guang Yu, Nod Young), Stanislaus Fung , Dai Chun , Gao Yan, Zhang Jian, Xin Zhong, Xiaowei Han, Yang Shi, Boxu Chen Commissario: China Arts & Entertainment Group (CAEG). Commissario Aggiunto: Zhang Yu. Curatore: Jiang Jun.

title: "Milan - The Rising City" [Venice - 28 June 2014]
tags: 2014 venice veneto nordest italy ve biennale exhibition biennale2014 14thvenicebiennale architecture venicebiennale fundamentals artwork installation arsenale 14thbiennale 14tharchitecturebiennale milantherisingcity skyscraper skyline milan model exhibit nikond5100
description: From the turn of the century, Milan's low horizon has been progressively modified by a number of higher buildings. The title of the 1911 "Futurist" painting by Umberto Boccioni, "The Rising City", anticipates the continuous dilemma and debate between innovation and permanence. Milan's professional culture has managed to produce high-rise buildings of noteworthy urban quality; if works like the Torre Galfa, the Torre Velasca and the Grattacielo Pirelli show different interpretation of the theme, the latest urban interventions seem to challenge once more the question of the scale of the city in relationship with its existing structure and with the larger territorial network.

title: Chinese Exhibit at Venice Biennale 2014
tags: venice architecture modernism pavilion biennale
description: There were these bundles of leaflets showing modernist architectures in China. In this corner, it is arranged along a time line. People can take a sheet. There were too many of them, though, so it was hard to get the whole picture, or their overall concept. The sheets were very small (A5), so there weren't enough details that made me want to take home. The floor was a combination of this flooring tiles on very low stools. It looked like some recycled materials but not sure.

title: 14th International Architecture Exhibition - La Biennale
tags: venice architecture exhibition international 14th venezia labiennale 14thinternationalarchitectureexhibition
description: Open to the public from 7 June to 23 November, 10 am to 6 pm (Arsenale venue 10 am to 8 pm on Fridays and Saturdays until 27 September) Fundamentals The 14th International Architecture Exhibition,entitled Fundamentals,directed by Rem Koolhaas and organized by la Biennale di Venezia, chaired by Paolo Baratta, will be open to the public from June 7 through November 23, 2014, in the Giardini and the Arsenale. The preview is on June 5 and 6 and the award ceremony and inauguration will take place on June 7. 65 National Participations will be exhibiting in the historic pavilions in the Giardini, the Arsenale, and the city of Venice. Among these, 10 countries will be participating in the Exhibition for the first time: Costa Rica, Dominican Republic, United Arab Emirates, Indonesia, Ivory Coast, Kenya, Morocco, Mozambique, New Zealand, and Turkey. www.labiennale.org/en/architecture/index.html

Among these, 10 countries will be participating in the Exhibition for the first time: Costa Rica, Dominican Republic, United Arab Emirates, Indonesia, Ivory Coast, Kenya, Morocco, Mozambique, New Zealand, and Turkey. www.labiennale.org/en/architecture/index.html

title: "Guge Bricks 1500" by Shu Yong [Venice - 7 September 2013]
tags: 2013 venice veneto nordest italy ve biennale exhibition biennale2013 55thvenicebiennale art venicebiennale 55thbiennale 55thartbiennale theencyclopedicpalace artwork installation arsenale gugebricks1500 shuyong bricks exhibit nikond5100
description:
(2013) 
Shu Yong solicited from the Chinese society 1500 slogans, words, maxims, quotations, mottoes, phrases, hot words in society and popular words on internet, and have them translated via Google into English, word by word. Then he wrote both the Chinese and the corresponding English on Chinese Xuan paper, and finally put them into 1500 transparent bricks in the same size as those of the Great Wall. They were named "Guge Bricks". Guge and Google are similar in pronunciation. The experience of Google in China has been an interesting phenomenon, which is the very result of the difference in view and value. The comprehension of the words, phrases about China's society, translated or rather "coined" by Google, is eventually an awkward, fragmentary, hazy process, in which one could really feel the gigantic differencesbetween the eastern and western cultures. The transparent Wall formed by 1500 "Guge Bricks" is one work full of inter-reactions of people, we encourage the viewers to write their understanding and their own translation of these Chinese words and phrases on the surface of the bricks.

title: "Fe2O3, Ossido Ferrico" by Francesca Grilli [Venice - 7 September 2013]
tags: 2013 venice veneto nordest italy ve biennale exhibition biennale2013 55thvenicebiennale art venicebiennale 55thbiennale 55thartbiennale theencyclopedicpalace artwork installation arsenale fe2o3ossidoferrico francescagrilli iron metal irondetails detalhesemferro rust exhibit sign nikond5100
description:
(2013) 

Francesca Grilli's work explores sound in its many expressive and perceptive implications, acting as an instrument for direct unmediated communication springing from the individual and collective unconsciuos. "Fe2O3, Ossido Ferrico" involves an on-site performer who interacts with the rhythm of water falling onto a large sheet of iron, by means of unscripted vocalizations. The prolonging of an acttion over a period of time translates into an enormously protracted gesture signifying a heroic princicple: a steadfast and self-possessed resistance to the end time. The relationshipbetween voice and material is developed by creating a bond between tha water and metal and the performer. While the erosion of the metal symbolizes the tangible powerof perseverance and reiteration, the voice, intended as a manifestation of the ultimate will to exist, is absorbed and amplified by the water. The rhythmic and incessant dripping of water on the metal, harmonizing with the singer's voice thus trasmits a message about the fragility of the unspoken word, simultaneously becoming a musical instrument and a sound box.

title: "The Dry Salvages" by Elisabetta Benassi I [Venice - 7 September 2013]
tags: 2013 venice veneto nordest italy ve biennale exhibition biennale2013 55thvenicebiennale art venicebiennale 55thbiennale 55thartbiennale theencyclopedicpalace artwork installation arsenale thedrysalvages elisabettabenassi masonry bricks clay exhibit nikond5100
description:
(2013) 

Elisabetta Benassi's work reflects on cultural, political and historical traditions of the 20th century, presentin pieces thatactas devices intended to stimulate strong emotional suggestions and prompt the viewer to contemplate the failure of modern ideologies. "The Dry Salvages" is presented as an uneven floor, composed of 10000 "bricks" made of clay taken from areas of the 1952 Polesine flood that are imprinted with the names and alphanumeric cataloguing codes of the largest pieces of space debris still in orbit around Earth. The artist's fascination with the inscrutability of the cosmic dimension is associated with the tangibility of an archaic, handcrafted product, and is evoked in a poetic way by referencing the homonymous T.S. Eliot poem. If this reflection on the inexorabilityof time and destructive power of nature reveals the possibility of a regeneration of matter, the large archive of space debris, to which the artist makes reference, calls to mind the "space race" of the second half of the 20th century, exposing its potentially catastrophic consequences at the same time.

"The Dry Salvages" is presented as an uneven floor, composed of 10000 "bricks" made of clay taken from areas of the 1952 Polesine flood that are imprinted with the names and alphanumeric cataloguing codes of the largest pieces of space debris still in orbit around Earth. The artist's fascination with the inscrutability of the cosmic dimension is associated with the tangibility of an archaic, handcrafted product, and is evoked in a poetic way by referencing the homonymous T.S. Eliot poem. If this reflection on the inexorabilityof time and destructive power of nature reveals the possibility of a regeneration of matter, the large archive of space debris, to which the artist makes reference, calls to mind the "space race" of the second half of the 20th century, exposing its potentially catastrophic consequences at the same time.

title: Le pavillon de la Chine (55ème Biennale de Venise)
tags: venice italy google italia biennale venise venezia italie dalbera biennaledevenise pavillondelachine anniedalbera
description: Guge Bricks 1500 (Guge = Google) Installation de l'artiste Shu Yong 2013 briques en résine Pavillon de la Chine 55ème Biennale de Venise L'artiste a recueilli 1500 slogans, mots, maximes et autres expressions utilisés dans le langage populaire chinois. Il a traduit ces mots en anglais avec Google Translation et les a inscrits sous forme calligraphique sur une feuille de papier de riz, à coté du mot chinois. Chaque papier a été incorporé dans une brique en résine dont la taille est la même que celles de la Grande Muraille de Chine. Les traductions par Google (Guge) sont très mauvaises ; selon l'artiste, elles révèlent les énormes différences qui existent, malgré la mondialisation, entre les cultures de l'est et de l'ouest. L’œuvre en forme de muraille symbolise ainsi le mur caché qui sépare les pays occidentaux de la Chine. En mettant en scène cette confrontation, l'installation tente d'abolir les frontières entre l'art et la société, dans cette perspective elle s'inscrit pleinement dans le thème du pavillon qui, en 2013, est la Transfiguration. artsy.net/post/chinesepavilion-about-the-artist-shu-yong www.designboom.com/art/shu-yong-great-wall-of-guge-bricks...

title: Le pavillon de la Chine (55ème Biennale de Venise)
tags: venice italy italia biennale venise venezia italie dalbera biennaledevenise pavillondelachine huistyle anniedalbera
description: Thing-in-itself, installation de Hu Yaolin, 2013 Bois sculpté Cette installation de l'artiste chinois Hu Yaolin utilise des éléments d'une maison traditionnelle chinoise ancienne de style Hui (style développé lors des Dynasties Song et Ming / Huizhou). En prenant cet exemple particulier, l'oeuvre interroge le visiteur sur les relations entre l'art d'aujourd'hui, les nouveaux espaces urbains et la tradition architecturale des villes d'autrefois. Selon le cartel signé par le commissaire Wang Chunchen, un des buts de cette installation est aussi de montrer au monde que les chinois conservent et s'intéressent à leur patrimoine architectural traditionnel malgré le développement économique très rapide que connait ce pays. La morphologie de ce type d'édifices avec son dôme et son puits de lumière ressemble à celle de certains bâtiments de la tradition romaine ou italienne qui ont été au cœur des pratiques constructives pendant des siècles tant en Chine que dans le sud de l'Europe. Transfiguration : thème du pavillon national de la Chine Artists: Group Show; Commissioner: China Arts and Entertainment Group; Curator: Wang Chunchen

title: Common Ground :: La Biennale 2012
tags: venice biennale venezia commonground labiennale architecturebiennale
description: Architecture Biennale 29th August > 25th November 2012 The 13th International Architecture Exhibition, directed by David Chipperfield and titled Common Ground, will be open from 29th August to 25th November 2012. David Chipperfield’s Common Ground The emphasis of the 2012 Biennale - explains David Chipperfield - is on what we have in common. Above all, the ambition of Common Ground is to reassert the existence of an architectural culture, made up not just of singular talents but a rich continuity of diverse ideas united in a common history, common ambitions, common predicaments and ideals. In architecture everything begins with the ground. It is our physical datum, where we make the first mark, digging the foundations that will support our shelter. www.labiennale.org/en/architecture/ Biennale Architettura 29 agosto > 25 novembre 2012 La 13. Mostra Internazionale di Architettura, diretta da David Chipperfield e intitolata Common Ground, sarà aperta al pubblico ai Giardini e all’Arsenale dal 29 agosto al 25 novembre 2012. Il Common Ground di David Chipperfield Il tema centrale di questa Biennale 2012 – spiega il Direttore David Chipperfield – è ciò che abbiamo in comune. L’ambizione di Common Ground è soprattutto quella di riaffermare l’esistenza di una cultura architettonica costituita non solo da singoli talenti, ma anche da un ricco patrimonio di idee differenti. Siamo partiti dal desiderio di enfatizzare idee condivise al di là della creazione individuale: questo ci imponeva di attivare dialoghi piuttosto che selezionare singoli partecipanti. www.labiennale.org/it/architettura

Sehr stark | Vin PSK: very beautiful very artistic!! | .Luigi Mirto/ArchiMlFotoWord FIAF/AFI-UIF: ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- Complimenti, ho visto questa tua bella immagine inserita nella tua galleria. Una ripresa molto interessante, ripresa ottimamente e con accortezza. . Mi scuso sempre se la mia presenza nel tuo stream è sporadico, ma dovendo visitare tutti i miei contatti, comprenderai la mole degli interventi. Grazie ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- . Rinnovo l’invito per visionare una nuova immagine che ho inserito nel mio Photo Stream. . Immagine tratta dal Racconto Reportage ”Volti e Usanze della gente Siciliana” ”Un racconto reportage che è stato effettuato nei paesi, borgate, casolari e contrade nell’entroterra Siciliana allo scopo di far conoscere le usanze, i modi, le attività sociali e culturali, della bellissima Sicilia”. In questa ripresa c’era la mamma che per addormentare il bimbo lo passeggiava lungo la strada sterrata lontano dall’abitazione e dai rumori di casa, il bimbo intanto al posto del ciuccio sgranocchiava una crosta di pane. In questo link l'ultima immagine inserita: www.flickr.com/photos/luigimirto/5591843877/in/photostream . . Image from the story Reportage ”Faces and Customs of the people of Sicily" "A narrative report that was made in the villages, hamlets, farmhouses and inland districts of Sicily in order to know the customs, manners, social and cultural activities, beautiful Sicily". In this shoot was the mother to sleep, the child was walking along the dirt road away from the house and the noises of the house, the baby pacifier in place while munching a crust of bread. . title: blue cityscape
tags: blue venice light italy abstract holland netherlands architecture model italia cityscape blu contemporary biennale astratto venezia architettura luce pavillion olanda polystyrene contemporaneo polistirolo veneto modello paesaggiourbano
description:
Biennale di Architettura 2010, Venezia.
Installazione all'interno del Padiglione Olanda.
comments:
tito santoni: Effettivamente una bella realizzazione e bella sistemazione.
Anche l'inquadratura mi pare perfetta.
Questa rappresentazione della realtà, questa occupazione dello spazio che diventa l'unico obiettivo, o sembra esserlo, mi turba molto, il mondo che si crea è freddo, mi respinge. Forse perché conservo un'anima barocca o, al più, neoclassica. | Enrico Casagni: Ma anch'io ci ho letto come una sorta di critica all'urbanizzazione incontrollata, tanto più da parte degli Olandesi che come saprai sono attentissimi a questo tema. Altro che noi. Più che pertinente quindi! | Alberto Cameroni: Nel senso che non c'è più nemmeno lo spazio per raggiungere l'uscita: la città come trappola... | buttha: in effetti è impressionante, come critica è sicuramente riuscita | sophystica: messaggio interessante e credo che la foto ne rappresenti bene il significato. bravo! | Giorgio Verdiani: molto bella nei toni e nella luce morbidissima, ben scattata, bravo. | cre_ationlinee'dp: avendo lavorato con gli olandesi, posso dar solo ragione...hanno una ragione incontrollata dell urbanism architettonico, cosa che mi ha fatto cambiar idea sulla mia architettura_ | leticiaperito: fotografia diferente, mais linda
